title: Event-loop scheduling core — run-to-idle, edge-triggered wake arbitration, and polling retirement
purpose: The event-loop scheduling core and F-0d gate governed by `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:59-72` at `32c69d4`. Today `run_until_idle` ticks native timers, takes one `ready.len()` snapshot, executes at most that many selections, and restores deferred yielders only after the loop (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:427-538`); its result contains executed/yielded/waiting/exited/errored but no true-idle, runnable-remaining, or native-deadline state (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:44-57`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:809-818`). The wrapper still exposes `pump_once`, a self-rescheduling rAF `start_pump`, and `PumpHandle` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:300-411`), while generated `runUntilExit` synchronously calls `run_step` up to a fixed maximum (`crates/beamr-wasm/build.rs:277-300`). Those are polling progress mechanisms forbidden by the binding ruling: “a polling scheduler is a TEAR CONDITION (F-0d acceptance). Any timer whose job is ‘check whether something changed’ is a design error” (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:17-23`). This brief replaces check-again progress with a drain result that says what happened, one coalesced host turn per externally-caused idle→runnable edge, and countable proof that a truly idle VM creates zero recurring host callbacks.
task:
Implement one coherent WPORT-2 scheduling change: evolve the cooperative scheduler into a bounded run-to-idle drain whose result distinguishes true idle, explicit fairness yield, and surfaced process errors; add an edge-triggered wrapper arbiter that schedules exactly one host turn per externally-caused idle→runnable transition; retire the rAF pump and synchronous generated completion loop; migrate every consumer and document; and add internal counters plus Node-runner acceptance that mechanically prove F-0d. The chosen host-turn primitive is DECIDED-AT-TEAR, not by the implementer. The implementation must probe that primitive once when installing the arbiter, refuse construction loudly if it is absent, and never discover or silently change mechanisms per scheduling request. Deviations — including apparently helpful WPORT-3 timer unification, new BIFs, registry changes, browser claims, or fallback chains not selected by the tear — STOP and report.

OPEN QUESTIONS FOR THE TEAR:
1. Arbiter mechanism: candidate A `queueMicrotask`, B Promise microtask/`spawn_local`, C `MessageChannel`, D `setTimeout(0)`, or E an explicit hybrid; candidates are non-binding and the tear decides with burst, ordering, rendering/fairness, and idle-wall evidence (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:198-200`).
2. Fairness-yield parameters: DRAFTER PROPOSAL is one reduction-bounded slice per selected PID, yielded PIDs held until the next host turn, and a production cap of 1,024 executed slices per drain before explicit fairness yield; the tear may replace the numeric cap or policy but must state the replacement.
3. Completion API naming/shape: DRAFTER PROPOSAL is `WasmVm::await_exit(pid) -> Promise` plus generated `awaitExit(vm, pid)`, resolving a structured exited/errored/true-idle outcome and never looping `run_step`; the tear owns the final public spelling and shape.
4. Environment refusal versus fallback: the brief default is one installation-time probe and loud constructor refusal naming the missing primitive. A fallback chain, its order, and its observability are a tear decision; no implementation-time fallback is permitted without that ruling.

requirements.title: Define a bounded run-to-idle drain result that reports idle, fairness yield, errors, and the next native deadline
requirements.spec: Replace the misleading one-snapshot/quiescence contract with a deliberate drain API while preserving `run_step` as the manually-invoked single-drain host entry. Current state is one initial budget snapshot plus deferred re-yield (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:427-538`), and the existing summary preserves errored PID identity (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:44-57`; error population at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:479-530`). The new result must carry three required outcome facts without conflation: (1) TRUE IDLE means no runnable work remains and includes `next_native_deadline: Option<web_time::Instant>` internally; the wrapper JSON exposes the same seam as a nullable non-negative milliseconds-from-now value computed once at serialization; (2) FAIRNESS YIELD means the host-turn policy stopped while runnable work remains and includes a positive exact `runnable_remaining` count; (3) errored PIDs remain surfaced exactly as identities and do not erase whether the post-drain state is idle or fairness-yielded. Add only `TimerWheel::earliest_deadline(&self) -> Option<web_time::Instant>` (or tear-approved equivalent) over the existing absolute `expires_at` values (`crates/beamr/src/timer.rs:52-65`; scheduling stores them at `crates/beamr/src/timer.rs:141-173`); the current public introspection is only `len`, `is_empty`, and `get(reference)` (`crates/beamr/src/timer.rs:221-237`), so this is an additive query, not a wheel redesign. Receive-after timers remain separate host-armed records carrying PID, opaque id, and relative milliseconds (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:24-33`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:618-633`; wrapper one-shots at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:427-478`) and do not enter this native-wheel deadline field. Preserve all four fairness walls: one reduction-bounded slice per selected bytecode PID (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:477-493`) or one native handler slice (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:316-347`); yielded/Continue processes held outside `ready` until the host turn ends (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:495-500`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:535-537`); strict Max→High→Normal→Low selection (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:682-710`, pinned at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native_tests.rs:1250-1301`); and bounded exposure to spawns/wakes arriving during the drain. DRAFTER PROPOSAL FOR TEAR: remove the initial-ready-length stop, continue selecting live ready work until empty or 1,024 executed slices, never reselect a yielded PID in the same host turn, then reinsert yielders and return FAIRNESS YIELD if either the cap was hit or reinserted/new ready work remains. Provide a test-only low-budget seam rather than making acceptance execute 1,024 slices. If the tear replaces any wall or parameter, the final spec/handoff names the replacement explicitly; silence is a deviation.
acceptance: `run_until_idle_reports_true_idle_and_earliest_native_deadline` pins TRUE IDLE twice: an empty scheduler reports no runnable work and `None`, while a scheduler with only a future native-wheel entry reports no runnable work and `Some(earliest)` equal to that entry; receive-after host records do not affect this field

`run_until_idle_reports_fairness_yield_with_runnable_remaining` uses the test-only low budget and a continuously-yielding/spawning workload to prove the drain returns FAIRNESS YIELD with a positive exact runnable-remaining count, does not wedge the host turn, and does not call one PID twice in the same turn
`run_until_idle_preserves_errored_pid_identity` proves an interpreter error still places the exact PID in `errored` while the result also reports the correct post-drain idle-or-yield state

The existing `run_until_idle_drains_ready_queue_in_priority_order` and re-yield accounting wall remain green (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native_tests.rs:1250-1343`), and new mid-drain spawn/wake coverage proves the configured fairness cap bounds host-turn work

The wrapper's manual `run_step`/drain JSON contains an explicit state discriminant, nullable native deadline, runnable-remaining count, and the existing five summary collections plus results; no boolean equivalent of `has_pending_work` is presented as the drain contract
No host callback is armed for the exposed native deadline in this requirement; arming, cancellation, re-arming, and timer-class unification remain WPORT-3 (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:74-87`)

requirements.title: Install an edge-triggered wake arbiter with one queued host turn and a tear-decided primitive
requirements.spec: Add a per-VM arbiter with explicit Idle, Queued, and Draining state. An externally-caused transition observed from empty-ready to runnable while the arbiter is Idle requests exactly one host turn and moves to Queued; further requests while Queued create no callback. Callback entry moves Queued→Draining. TRUE IDLE moves Draining→Idle so the next edge can queue one new turn; FAIRNESS YIELD schedules exactly one immediate continuation and returns to Queued. The decisive ready-empty-before observation belongs at the Rust mutation sites, where `ready.len()` is an O(1) sum of four queues (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:682-710`), while the selected host primitive call belongs in the wasm wrapper. Cover every host-entry producer that can make an idle VM runnable: `send_message` reaches `send_owned`/`after_successful_enqueue` and the `wake` push (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:118-127`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:300-360`); exported bytecode `spawn` reaches the direct ready push (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:175-188`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:257-297`); the spawn category also covers host-created actor/native roots and transient call clients because `spawn_actor` and `call` converge on `spawn_native_root` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:218-265`; `crates/beamr/src/native/actor_cooperative.rs:167-197`, `crates/beamr/src/native/actor_cooperative.rs:334-360`; ready push at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:192-206`); `cast` specifically creates a transient native root (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:267-278`; `crates/beamr/src/native/actor_cooperative.rs:140-165`); Promise/async completion covers the bytecode wake and native envelope paths (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:176-195`; wrapper completion at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:556-579`), including the native heap-copy-failure fallback wake (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:209-249`); and receive-timer fire enters `timer_fired` and wakes (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:158-174`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:456-478`). In-turn producers do not independently queue a JS callback: deferred native sends/child spawns are materialized before the active slice returns (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:350-453`), and trapped-exit delivery enqueues/wakes during exit propagation (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:456-507`); the active drain must observe them and either consume them or return FAIRNESS YIELD. MECHANISM DECIDED-AT-TEAR: the following candidates are non-binding options, not implementation permission. A — `queueMicrotask` through the existing dynamic-global `js_sys::global`/`Reflect`/`Function`/`Closure` pattern (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:29-34`, analogous helpers at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:835-888`); no queueMicrotask implementation exists. B — Promise microtask/`spawn_local`; `js-sys` and `wasm-bindgen-futures` are direct dependencies (`crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:22-29`), and Promise/`JsFuture`/`spawn_local` are already used (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:29-34`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:556-579`), but host ordering/starvation is unmeasured. C — `MessageChannel`; no binding/use exists and `web-sys` is absent from this crate's direct normal dependencies (`crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:13-29`), so a typed path adds a dependency/feature and an untyped constructor path remains unproved. D — `setTimeout(0)`; the existing global helper accepts a zero-capable delay but requires a numeric return handle (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:835-850`). UNVERIFIED-ON-PLATFORM: the local Node probe returns an object handle, so the current helper cannot be assumed Node-compatible; the repository claim is only the numeric assumption. The repository test comment says the existing `start_pump` rAF loop is browser-only and does not fire under the bare wasm runner (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:957-966`); broader Node/browser/Worker rAF availability remains UNVERIFIED-ON-PLATFORM. E — explicit microtask/macrotask hybrid; the governing paper permits it but leaves starvation/fairness to the tear (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:198-200`), and R1 supplies the required explicit fairness signal. Recurring rAF is excluded, not a candidate (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:315-380`). At arbiter installation during `WasmVm::new` or an immediately-called setup, probe the tear-selected primitive exactly once, retain the callable/capability, and return a loud constructor-time error naming the missing primitive. Default to refusal: no per-request lookup, silent fallback, or runtime mechanism switching. Browser/Worker ordering, throttling, and availability remain UNVERIFIED-ON-PLATFORM; the executed acceptance ground is the Node wasm-bindgen command already present in CI (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:21-37`). NO-POLLING is R2 acceptance: a polling scheduler is a TEAR CONDITION, and any timer whose job is ‘check whether something changed’ is wrong (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:19-23`, `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:68-70`).
acceptance: `idle_to_runnable_burst_queues_one_arbiter_turn` starts from TRUE IDLE, performs N sends before yielding to the host, and observes exactly one arbiter host-turn request, queued-now = 1, and one execution
`arbiter_reedges_after_true_idle` proves that after the queued turn drains and returns to TRUE IDLE, one later send queues and executes exactly one additional turn

`arbiter_installation_rejects_missing_host_primitive` removes or replaces the tear-selected primitive before VM construction and asserts a constructor-time error that names that primitive; no scheduling call is attempted and no fallback is selected

A table-driven core/wrapper test covers send, bytecode spawn, actor/native-root spawn including `call` and `cast`, async completion's bytecode and native paths including heap-copy fallback, and receive-timer fire; each external idle→runnable edge reaches the arbiter exactly once
Separate in-turn coverage proves deferred child spawn/send and trapped-exit wake are consumed by the active drain or produce the single fairness continuation, never a duplicate external turn
At all times there is at most one queued arbiter callback per VM; a fairness continuation uses the same coalescing state, and TRUE IDLE with no external edge schedules nothing
Acceptance is mechanism-agnostic and Node-runner-only: no assertion depends on rAF, Window, Worker, rendering, browser throttling, or an unverified host ordering guarantee

requirements.title: Retire both polling drivers and replace synchronous completion with an awaitable arbiter-driven API
requirements.spec: Delete the live `WasmVm::pump_once`, `WasmVm::start_pump`, `PumpHandle`, `FrameCell`, private `pump_turn`, and rAF request/cancel helpers. At `32c69d4`, exhaustive tracked-file searches `git grep -n -w pump_once`, `git grep -n -E 'start_pump|PumpHandle'`, and `git grep -n -w pump_turn` find no tracked production consumer: definitions/callers/tests are confined to the wrapper (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:300-425`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:957-1017`) and recommendations to the legacy design (`docs/WASM-RUNTIME-PORT-DESIGN.md:507-564`). Keep `run_step` as the explicit manual single-drain entry implementing R1; one embedder-invoked drain is event-driven hosting, not polling. Replace generated synchronous `runUntilExit`, which loops over `run_step` and reads `summary.results/executed/waiting/yielded` (`crates/beamr-wasm/build.rs:277-300`), with the tear-approved awaitable completion API riding arbiter/exit notification and containing no bounded or unbounded step loop. DRAFTER PROPOSAL FOR TEAR: `WasmVm::await_exit(pid) -> Promise` and generated `awaitExit(vm, pid)` resolve immediately if the target result is already retained; otherwise they resolve `{ state: 'exited', pid, result, summary }` on target exit, `{ state: 'errored', pid, result: null, summary }` on surfaced target error identity, or `{ state: 'idle', pid, result: null, summary }` if the VM reaches TRUE IDLE before that target completes. FAIRNESS YIELD never resolves the Promise; it requests the one coalesced continuation. Wrapper/scheduler/arbiter errors reject the Promise. The API consumes or deliberately documents retention of the same exit result exactly once; no `maxSteps` option survives. Migrate the production edge worker, which currently imports/calls `runUntilExit` and reads the polling-only `BEAMR_EDGE_MAX_STEPS` knob (`examples/edge-worker/src/worker.js:1-20`, `examples/edge-worker/src/worker.js:77-89`); remove the corresponding configured binding from `examples/edge-worker/wrangler.toml:6-9`, `await` the replacement, and leave no max-step configuration surface. Migrate its in-test bundle stub and import replacement (`examples/edge-worker/test/worker.test.mjs:6-53`) so the HTTP adapter test keeps matching the public API, while retaining the boundary that a JS stub is not scheduler acceptance. Update its README contract (`examples/edge-worker/README.md:17-24`, `examples/edge-worker/README.md:39-56`). Update or explicitly supersede the legacy pump/rAF recommendation (`docs/WASM-RUNTIME-PORT-DESIGN.md:503-564`) with the R1/R2 event-driven contract; a live recommendation for a tear condition may not remain. Every summary-shape consumer moves in the same commit: the serializer (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:809-818`), generated bootstrap (`crates/beamr-wasm/build.rs:277-293`), scheduler tests that inspect fields, and the edge stub's summary lookup (`examples/edge-worker/test/worker.test.mjs:22-48`). NO-POLLING is R3 acceptance: no recurring rAF/timer and no synchronous `run_step` loop may remain a progress mechanism (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:19-23`, `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:64-70`).
acceptance: No production export, implementation, or call of `pump_once`, `start_pump`, `PumpHandle`, `FrameCell`, `pump_turn`, `request_animation_frame`, or `cancel_animation_frame` remains under `crates/beamr-wasm` or `examples`; historical governing-paper/brief mentions may remain, but `docs/WASM-RUNTIME-PORT-DESIGN.md` no longer recommends them
`run_step` remains callable as one manual R1 drain and returns the complete new result; no manual-drive documentation presents repeated calls as a completion or idle protocol
Generated output exports the tear-approved awaitable completion API and no `runUntilExit`/`maxSteps` loop; source and generated tests prove it never calls `run_step` recursively or repeatedly

`await_vm_call_resolves_with_js_handler_reply` no longer performs its eight-call manual loop and still reaches the real actor/JS-handler Promise result `42` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:904-955` at the base)

The pump test is migrated and renamed `arbiter_drives_an_actor_call_to_completion`; it contains no `pump_once` loop or optional rAF handle and still reaches the real actor/Promise result `8` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:957-1017` at the base)

The edge worker awaits completion; `DEFAULT_MAX_STEPS`, `configuredMaxSteps`, `BEAMR_EDGE_MAX_STEPS`, and the wrangler variable binding are absent from `examples/edge-worker/src/worker.js` and `examples/edge-worker/wrangler.toml`; `npm test --prefix examples/edge-worker` preserves its HTTP 201/header/body, 426 WebSocket, and exit-result cleanup walls (`examples/edge-worker/test/worker.test.mjs:56-110`)
All summary-shape consumers compile/test in the same commit; there is no compatibility period where generated JS, the worker, tests, or documentation interpret the old result shape

requirements.title: Make the idle wall countable and permanent in the Node wasm test gate
requirements.spec: Add arbiter-internal instrumentation owned by each VM and route every progress-turn scheduling request through it, independent of which tear-selected primitive implements the callback. The snapshot must expose: cumulative arbiter host-turn `requests`, `queued_now` as a 0/1 gauge, cumulative `executions`, and cumulative `cancellations`. Count permitted known-deadline one-shots in a separately-labelled group — requests/queued-now/executions/cancellations for receive timers now and the WPORT-3 native deadline service later — so deadline delivery can never be confused with an arbiter progress turn. DRAFTER CHOICE: visibility is a non-wasm-exported Rust snapshot accessor in a separate `#[cfg(all(test, target_arch = "wasm32"))]` `impl WasmVm`; the in-crate wasm-bindgen tests can read it, while the production JS ABI gains no diagnostic method. A non-cancellable selected primitive may report zero cancellations, but the field remains present and queued-now must return to zero on execution/teardown. The idle-wall test settles the real VM to R1 TRUE IDLE, snapshots counters, awaits one host macrotask, and asserts zero new arbiter requests/executions and queued-now = 0; permitted known-deadline counts are asserted separately rather than waved away. Add the five outline acceptance walls under the pinned Node runner: IDLE WALL; COALESCING; RE-EDGE; FAIRNESS; and MIGRATED WALLS preserving result 42, result 8, and the three conversion tests byte-untouched (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:904-1017`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/convert.rs:390-501`). Also carry R2's missing-primitive constructor test. The current workflow executes the locked Node runner and machine-asserts five exact names and an exact five-pass summary (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:21-67`). Update that exact-name array and exact summary count in the same commit as the tests. DRAFTER EXPECTED LIST if the proposed names survive the tear: the three existing conversion names; `tests::await_vm_call_resolves_with_js_handler_reply`; renamed `tests::arbiter_drives_an_actor_call_to_completion`; `tests::idle_vm_schedules_zero_recurring_callbacks`; `tests::idle_to_runnable_burst_queues_one_arbiter_turn`; `tests::arbiter_reedges_after_true_idle`; `tests::fairness_yield_reports_runnable_remaining`; and `tests::arbiter_installation_rejects_missing_host_primitive`, for exactly 10 passed. If the tear changes names/count, the workflow must assert the final exact inventory and exact summary, still failing on zero discovery. Perform, record, and revert three fail-first mutations: (a) reintroduce a self-rescheduling recurring progress driver through the instrumented scheduling seam and show `idle_vm_schedules_zero_recurring_callbacks` fails; (b) disable coalescing so every request queues and show `idle_to_runnable_burst_queues_one_arbiter_turn` fails; (c) feed the workflow assertion captured passing output with one stale/missing expected name and show the job fails. NO-POLLING is R4 acceptance and F-0d: “a polling scheduler is a TEAR CONDITION ... Any timer whose job is ‘check whether something changed’ is a design error” (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:19-23`); the idle wall must catch recurring rAF, interval, timeout, microtask, macrotask, hybrid continuation, or synchronous recheck behavior, not merely the selected happy path.
acceptance: `idle_vm_schedules_zero_recurring_callbacks` reaches TRUE IDLE on the real VM, snapshots internal counters, awaits a macrotask, and observes no increase in arbiter requests/executions and queued-now = 0; this is the F-0d IDLE WALL
`idle_to_runnable_burst_queues_one_arbiter_turn` proves a burst of N sends from idle produces exactly one queued and one executed arbiter turn, with no duplicate callback
`arbiter_reedges_after_true_idle` proves the queued flag clears after execution/idle and the next idle→runnable edge produces exactly one new request/execution

`fairness_yield_reports_runnable_remaining` proves a continuous yield/spawn workload hits the explicit boundary, reports positive runnable-remaining, and progresses through coalesced continuation turns without wedging or silently spinning

Migrated walls stay real and green: handler reply test returns `42`; renamed arbiter-driven actor-call test returns `8`; `converts_complex_nested_js_object_to_term`, `converts_terms_to_js_values`, and `documents_boolean_atom_round_trip_as_atom_names` remain byte-untouched (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/convert.rs:390-501`)
Arbiter and known-deadline counters are separately named and asserted; an idle-wall pass cannot be manufactured by subtracting or ignoring unclassified callbacks

`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml` asserts every final wasm-bindgen test name and the exact final passed/failed/ignored/filtered summary in the same commit; a missing name, stale name, wrong count, or `no tests to run!` fails mechanically

The handoff contains reverted fail-first evidence for (a) recurring self-rescheduling progress, (b) disabled coalescing, and (c) stale CI name-list output, plus 10 consecutive isolated passes of every new or changed acceptance pin

boundaries: NO timer-service unification or host deadline arming — WPORT-3's scope. This brief only exposes the earliest native deadline in the drain result (additive TimerWheel query).
NO new BIFs; no registry/registration-semantics changes; no native (threaded) scheduler changes beyond the additive TimerWheel query it shares.
NO threads-feature surface changes; wasm closure stays cooperative,json.
NO JS-stub substitution in tests (arc wall); real VM, real wasm execution.
NO browser/Worker platform claims in acceptance (UNVERIFIED-ON-PLATFORM until the platform briefs); Node runner is the executed ground.
Untouchable: crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/** and awl-bc1-* branches (Vesper's claim).

Laws, stated where they bind: NO-POLLING is THE acceptance of R2/R3/R4 (F-0d gate — a polling scheduler is a tear condition; any timer whose job is "check whether something changed" is wrong); believed-state-is-not-citable-state binds every claim (file:line at 32c69d4, re-verified bytes, never the ground-pack artifact).
verification: Run the house bar on the final head: `cargo fmt --check`; touch every modified Rust file and run fresh `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`; run fresh `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`; run `cargo test --workspace --all-features --lib`; run `cargo test --workspace --all-features --test '*'`; and run `npm test --prefix examples/edge-worker`. Run both locked wasm checks retained at `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:17-20`: `cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p beamr --no-default-features --features cooperative,json --locked` and `cargo check --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked`. Then run the pinned executed leg from `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:21-67`: `CARGO_TARGET_WASM32_UNKNOWN_UNKNOWN_RUNNER=wasm-bindgen-test-runner cargo test --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked -- --nocapture`, and require the updated machine assertion to find every exact final name plus the exact final summary; if the drafter's proposed inventory is retained, require exactly 10 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored, 0 filtered out. Run every new or changed acceptance pin in isolation 10 consecutive times, including the idle-wall macrotask hop, coalescing, re-edge, fairness, missing-primitive refusal, migrated 42, and migrated 8 tests. Record and revert fail-first proofs: (a) a self-rescheduling recurring progress callback makes the idle wall fail; (b) unconditional queueing makes coalescing fail; (c) a stale expected-name list makes the workflow assertion fail. Stamp the handoff with final `git rev-parse HEAD`, `rustc --version --verbose`, `cargo --version`, `wasm-bindgen-test-runner --version`, `node --version`, wasm target, exact commands, UTC timestamps, selected arbiter primitive and one-time probe result, final fairness constants, final completion API spelling/shape, counter snapshots, exact test inventory, and fail-first outputs. No browser/Worker claim is accepted by this block.
